event: Madonna and Child with Saint Jerome and Mary Magdalene
symbols: Virgin nursing the Christ Child — the intimacy of the Incarnation, God made flesh and nourished by human care
Saint Jerome with book — the great Doctor of the Church and translator of Scripture into Latin
Mary Magdalene kneeling — the penitent sinner transformed by Christ's forgiveness
angel attending the scene — heavenly witness to the sacred gathering
landscape vista — the created world over which Christ is sovereign
description: A richly colored composition showing the Virgin Mary at center, nursing or holding the infant Christ. To the left, an elderly semi-nude bearded man (Jerome) holds a book or manuscript. A kneeling young woman in golden robes reaches toward the child from the right. An angelic figure with wings accompanies the group. Behind them, a draped canopy or curtain partially covers a distant landscape. The painting is warm in tone with deep reds, greens, and golden hues.
theological_description: This devotional gathering brings together figures from different periods of sacred history around the Madonna and Child, a common convention in sacra conversazione paintings. Jerome (c. 347-420), whose Latin Vulgate translation made Scripture accessible to the Western Church, represents the scholarly contemplation of the Word made flesh. His presence with a book echoes John's prologue: 'In the beginning was the Word' (JHN.1.1), for Jerome devoted his life to transmitting that Word faithfully. Mary Magdalene, the forgiven sinner who became the first witness of the Resurrection (JHN.20.11-18), represents the transformative power of Christ's mercy. The nursing Madonna emphasizes the full humanity of the Incarnation: the eternal God 'emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, being born in the likeness of men' (PHP.2.7). Together, these figures represent the Church in its fullness — the contemplative, the penitent, and the faithful mother — all gathered around the incarnate Word.

scripture_refs.reason: The Word made flesh, the theological center of the Incarnation celebrated in this gathering
Mary Magdalene as witness of the Resurrection, present here in devotion to the infant Christ
Christ emptying himself and taking human form, shown in the nursing Madonna
The Magdalene's many sins forgiven because she loved much
All Scripture is God-breathed, the principle that guided Jerome's life work of translation

_meta.title: The Madonna of Saint Jerome (after Correggio)
